paper_title: Tree-Ring Width and Stable Carbon Isotope Analysis

methods.description: Process for collecting and analyzing tree-ring width (TRW) samples to study growth patterns and environmental influences.

steps.description: Sampled 11 trees and collected 21 cores on the Murina site and 16 trees and 27 cores on the Hridsko site.
steps.searchable_summary: collect tree core samples from specified sites

steps.description: Cores were stored in plastic holders and transported to the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: store and transport tree core samples to lab

steps.description: TRW cores were dried, glued onto wooden holders, and sanded with progressively finer sanding paper.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare tree core samples for analysis

steps.description: Scanned both types of tree core using the ATRICS system; TRW measured using CooRecorder and CDendro programs.
steps.searchable_summary: scan and measure tree core samples

steps.description: Standardized individual TRW series using a 67% cubic smoothing spline with a 50% frequency cut-off in the dplR library.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize TRW series using cubic smoothing spline

steps.description: Calculated departures from the regression curve and pre-whitened index values using an autoregressive model.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate departures and pre-whiten index values

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing stable carbon isotope ratios (δ 13 C) in tree rings to study environmental changes.

steps.description: Isolated α-cellulose from wood slivers using chemical baths.
Homogenized cellulose with ultrasound, froze at −80 °C, and freeze-dried.
Combusted cellulose samples and analyzed using a mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze cellulose samples with mass spectrometer

steps.description: Applied atmospheric and PIN corrections to δ 13 C series.
steps.searchable_summary: apply corrections to δ 13 C series

methods.description: Analysis of the relationship between climate variables and tree growth using TRW and δ 13 C data.

steps.description: Calculated the expressed population signal (EPS) to assess the common signal in chronologies.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate EPS for common signal assessment

steps.description: Compared TRW and δ 13 C chronologies to climate variables using bootstrapped correlation coefficients.
Investigated spatial correlation patterns using temperature and precipitation gridded data.
